segments.text: Forasmuch as many have taken in hand to set forth in order a declaration of those things which are most surely believed among us,
Even as they delivered them unto us, which from the beginning were eyewitnesses, and ministers of the word;
It seemed good to me also, having had perfect understanding of all things from the very first, to write unto thee in order, most excellent Theophilus,
That thou mightest know the certainty of those things, wherein thou hast been instructed.
There was in the days of Herod, the king of Judæa, a certain priest named Zacharias, of the course of Abia: and his wife was of the daughters of Aaron, and her name was Elisabeth.

heading: The Birth of John the Baptist

segments.text: But the angel said unto him,
Fear not, Zacharias: for thy prayer is heard; and thy wife Elisabeth shall bear thee a son, and thou shalt call his name John.
segments.speaker: Angel

segments.text: And Zacharias said unto the angel,
Whereby shall I know this? for I am an old man, and my wife well stricken in years.
segments.speaker: Zacharias

segments.text: And the angel came in unto her, and said,
Hail, thou that art highly favoured, the Lord is with thee: blessed art thou among women.
segments.speaker: Angel

segments.text: Then said Mary unto the angel,
How shall this be, seeing I know not a man?
segments.speaker: Mary

segments.text: Behold the handmaid of the Lord; be it unto me according to thy word.
segments.speaker: Mary

segments.text: And the angel departed from her.
My soul doth magnify the Lord,
segments.speaker: Mary

segments.text: And my spirit hath rejoiced in God my Saviour.
segments.speaker: Mary

segments.text: And his father Zacharias was filled with the Holy Ghost, and prophesied, saying,
Blessed be the Lord God of Israel; for he hath visited and redeemed his people,
segments.speaker: Zacharias
